summary: Route a focused client_engineer repair after the test_engineer R2 PARTIAL handoff proved the smaller viewport still hides the map and map legend from the first screen.
acceptance_criteria: The smaller supported viewport evidence must show identity/situation/objective/recommended action, actionable task rail summary, map surface, and map legend in the actual first viewport.
The test must assert viewport intersection or visibility rather than DOM presence only.
The fix must preserve the accepted task rail intent and must not hide player-critical actions to make the map pass.
The fix must not approve or reopen M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001.

decisions.decision: The validation result is not routed to QA as ACCEPT because the 1280x720 and 1280x800 evidence fails the first-screen map/legend claim.
A focused client_engineer support repair is routed inside M7-UX-RECOVERY-VALIDATION-001 because the failing evidence is responsive layout and first-screen visibility, not a new product decision.
Formal content lock remains not accepted until this validation task is repaired, independently reviewed, integrated, and then content lock is reconsidered.

risks.summary: A layout can technically contain all player UI elements while still hiding the map and legend below the first viewport, reproducing the product-owner complaint.

requested_action: Repair the responsive first-screen UX evidence gap, update viewport-visible Playwright coverage and screenshots, run required tests, then write the client repair REVIEW or PARTIAL/BLOCKED handoff.
